purpose: Iteration-8 honest scoping: can ANY powered external validation survive the REAL committed pipeline (trust score / binary gate / coverage), non-surrogate, non-self-concordant, without wet-lab? And what is the true maximum honest merit gain?
honesty_contract: No surrogate/adjudication columns; scored the committed continuous trust score and the committed binary gate rule directly; nulls reported plainly.

A_trust_score_at_scale.cost: pooled-proxy trust (committed trust.py path for non-donor-pair genes) = 22s for 11,526 genes on 1.5MB parquet; base-pred sd=0.047. Donor-blocked path needs 16GB h5mu but only ~2591-gene reproducible core; also tractable. Computing trust for a large gene set is NOT the bottleneck.
A_trust_score_at_scale.test: continuous trust (0..1) scored vs frozen external 98-gene drug-target labels (day7 expanded)
A_trust_score_at_scale.interpretation: Trust is AT/BELOW chance (and slightly ANTI-correlated) with external target validity — immune drug targets have LOWER trust. This is correct behavior: trust measures per-perturbation predictive confidence (model agreement / effect reproducibility), which is ORTHOGONAL to therapeutic merit. The trust score therefore CANNOT yield a powered external biology win; the powered finding here is the confirmed orthogonality (trust is non-binding, now measured at n=98 external).

B_binary_gate_at_universe_scale.rule: GO iff ot_t1d>=0.20 AND eqtl_nsig>0 (committed gate reproduction; trust non-binding at scale, leakage-clean by construction)

vs_drug_target_literal_tier2.note: literal tier confounded: 38/56 positives are metformin->complex-I(NDUF) only. Below chance.

vs_drug_target_immune_tier.note: directionally positive, right sign, but UNDERPOWERED (7 positives, p=0.22). This is the honest 'real gate' drug-target number (not the retracted 0.829 surrogate).

B_binary_gate_at_universe_scale.verdict: No surviving powered win. Literal tier below chance; immune tier positive-but-underpowered.

C_other_axes.coloc_QTL_T1D: AUROC 0.638 < ot-alone baseline 0.701; perm_p=0.57 -> no win (day0_orthogonal_genetics_receipt).
C_other_axes.clinical_outcome_direction: right-signed, gate WITHHELD the 1 documented failure, but n_failure=1 (structurally empty minority class), Fisher p=0.375 -> unpowerable (day8_outcome_direction).
C_other_axes.external_calibration_transfer: frozen T1D quantiles COLLAPSE on Norman K562 (cov 0.05/0.08/0.17); re-fitting identical split-conformal on Norman's own calib split restores near-nominal (0.85/0.89/0.94, within +/-0.05 band). METHOD generalizes; specific quantiles are assay-bound. This is the ONE genuine non-surrogate positive but it is n-of-1 external dataset.

candidate_powered_axis.name: Multi-dataset conformal calibration-transfer (method-generalization coverage claim)
candidate_powered_axis.why_it_is_the_only_powered_non_surrogate: Coverage is measured at n=10^4-10^5 rows (tight Clopper-Pearson), the claim ('split-conformal recalibration restores near-nominal coverage on an independent assay') is non-surrogate and NOT self-concordant (external data the pipeline never saw), and it CAN fail. Currently proven on 1 external dataset (Norman K562). Extending to 3-5 additional public Perturb-seq datasets (e.g. Replogle K562/RPE1, Frangieh, Papalexi, Jost) would convert an n-of-1 case study into a powered methodological claim: 'conformal calibration transfers across N independent Perturb-seq assays under per-assay recalibration.'
candidate_powered_axis.feasibility: In-scope: public GEO/figshare matrices, streamed (machinery already exists in conformal_shift.py + the Norman pipeline). ~1-2 days engineering. No wet-lab.
candidate_powered_axis.honest_merit_value: Solid incremental methods, well-powered (65-79 band) but at the LOWER end: the selective-prediction utility is real yet the absolute AURC lift over null is tiny (0.003), so this validates a calibration wrapper rather than discovering biology.

verdict.reason: Every external BIOLOGY axis has a structurally empty/near-empty minority class (drug immune-tier n_pos=7 p=0.22; clinical failures n=1; coloc loses to baseline; mouse-KO dead). The committed continuous trust score is orthogonal-to-merit by design (AUROC 0.26-0.31, below chance). Power exists ONLY on the calibration-COVERAGE axis, which is a statistical property of the method, not a biological validation.
verdict.achievable_powered_win: YES but only methodological: the multi-dataset conformal-transfer coverage claim.
verdict.max_honest_merit_gain: +6 to +10 (from ~52 to ~58-62), realized by the multi-dataset transfer power-up + presenting the trust-orthogonality-at-scale null as a measured feature. NOT +20.
verdict.why_72_is_out_of_reach_in_scope: Nobel-anchored merit >65 requires validated, well-powered, NOVEL science. A well-calibrated trust wrapper with a small selective-prediction lift caps in the low-60s no matter how cleanly validated, because it does not itself discover or change biology. Closing the gap to 72 requires PROSPECTIVE wet-lab validation of the GO nominations (CD226 / ZFP36L1) — out of scope.
